hero.title: On-chain программы NiceChunk, IDs и границы интеграции.
hero.subtitle: Каталог для игроков и разработчиков о текущих Solana программах NiceChunk: что хранит каждый контракт, что вызывает frontend и что пока вне области devnet.
hero.networkNote: Эти контракты нужны для разработки и frontend-интеграции. Они не означают финальность mainnet.

directory.title: Текущие программы NiceChunk.
directory.body: Таблица показывает контракты в репозитории и текущую devnet поверхность интеграции.

flow.title: Как текущий игровой путь касается chain.

steps.title: Читать genesis config
steps.body: The client derives GlobalConfig from the core program and reads world seed, chunk size, build limits, NCK mint, and published config hashes.

steps.title: Создать профиль игрока
steps.body: The player program stores wallet-owned profile data, default attributes, position, equipment slots, and backpack style.

steps.title: Открыть mining session
steps.body: A player can create a short-lived session authority so repeated mining actions do not require wallet confirmation every block.

steps.title: Записать сломанные блоки
steps.body: The chunk program verifies the generated single block, then writes a compact 3-byte broken-block record into the chunk-broken PDA.

steps.title: Добавить ресурсы
steps.body: The backpack program is prepared to append mined resource records after session validation. It is the inventory-side bridge for the mining loop.

steps.title: Smelt Resources Into Items
steps.body: The smelting program reads a recipe table PDA, validates selected backpack input and fuel slots, removes those slots through the backpack program, and appends material or item outputs back into the same backpack through the smelting authority PDA.

steps.title: List Resources or Assets
steps.body: The marketplace verifies either a backpack resource slot or a market Asset PDA, then locks the item inside a listing PDA before it can be bought.

steps.title: Settle Market Orders
steps.body: A buyer pays in NCK or SOL, the program routes a 1% market fee to treasury, and the resource or Asset ownership is delivered to the buyer.

details.title: Код, instructions, PDA seeds и хранимые данные.

developer.title: Ссылки для frontend и scripts.
developer.boundaryBody: This page documents the live devnet contract surface. It does not claim final mainnet governance, land ownership, complete realtime decentralization, or complete gameplay decentralization.
developer.pdaCode:
GlobalConfig: ["global-config"]
PlayerProfile: ["player", owner]
PlayerSession: ["session", owner, sessionAuthority]
ChunkState: ["chunk", globalConfig, chunkX:i32_le, chunkZ:i32_le]
ChunkBrokenState: ["chunk-broken", globalConfig, chunkX:i32_le, chunkZ:i32_le]
GuardianRegistry: ["guardian-registry", globalConfig]
GuardianRegion: ["guardian-region", globalConfig, regionX:i32_le, regionY:i32_le]
GuardianTreasuryAuthority: ["guardian-treasury", globalConfig]
Backpack: ["backpack", creator, backpackId:u64_le]
RecipeTable: ["smelting-recipes", tableId:u64_le]
SmeltingAuthority: ["smelting-authority"]
MarketplaceListing: ["listing", seller, listingId:u64_le]
MarketAsset: ["asset", owner, assetId:u64_le]
MarketplaceAuthority: ["market-authority"]

programs.name: NiceChunk Genesis Core
programs.role: Immutable world configuration and NCK mint validation.
programs.summary: The core program initializes the GlobalConfig PDA once. It writes compiled genesis parameters such as world seed, world ID, config hashes, NCK mint, NCK decimals, genesis supply, fees, chunk limits, and guardian parameters. It does not mint items, manage inventory, update admin settings, or run gameplay logic.
instructions: Validates SPL Token mint layout, decimals, initialized flag, genesis supply, disabled mint authority, and disabled freeze authority.
Creates or allocates the GlobalConfig PDA safely even if the PDA was pre-funded with dust lamports.
storage: World ID and 32-byte world seed.
Terrain, resource rule, and client world config hashes.
NCK mint, decimals, genesis supply, and development wallet.
Chunk size, section height, build limits, max terrain height, sea level, cooldowns, and guardian settings.
notes: This is the immutable genesis config layer for the current devnet build.
Future crafting, inventory, chunk state, world knowledge, and reputation systems belong in separate programs or future modules after PDA specs are finalized.
Devnet and testnet must use their own NCK mint; the mainnet mint should not be reused on development clusters.

pdaAccounts.name: GlobalConfig
pdaAccounts.purpose: Immutable genesis configuration for the current world.

programs.name: NiceChunk Player
programs.role: Wallet-owned player profile, position, equipment slots, backpack style, and mining session authority.
programs.accountSize: PlayerProfile: 449 bytes; legacy PlayerProfile: 417 bytes; PlayerSession: 184 bytes
programs.summary: The player program creates a wallet-bound profile and a short-lived session authority. The session lets the game submit repeated allowed actions, such as block breaking, without opening the wallet for every single mined block.
storage: Owner wallet, GlobalConfig address, world ID, bump, and timestamps.
Base attributes: health, energy, stamina, mining power, build power, and defense.
Current position and nine equipment slots.
Session authority, allowed action bitmask, expiration time, max actions, and action count.
notes: The profile is public state. It stores the equipped backpack public key, while private backpack contents are loaded separately when the player opens inventory flows.
The current session model is used to reduce repeated wallet prompts during mining.
Sessions are bounded by expiration, allowed action flags, and max action count.

pdaAccounts.name: PlayerProfile
pdaAccounts.purpose: Wallet-owned public player state and equipped backpack pointer.
fields: base health, energy, stamina and power fields

pdaAccounts.name: PlayerSession
pdaAccounts.purpose: Short-lived gameplay authority for repeated allowed actions.

programs.name: NiceChunk Chunk
programs.role: Verifies generated single blocks and records compact broken-block state per chunk.
programs.accountSize: ChunkState: 8,280 bytes; ChunkBrokenState: 16-byte header + 3 bytes per record
programs.summary: The chunk program supports legacy chunk delta accounts and the current optimized mining path. For mining, the player submits one world block coordinate. The program derives the chunk, verifies the generated block, rejects air/water/bedrock or already-mined positions, and appends a compressed broken-block record.
storage: Legacy ChunkState stores up to 128 64-byte block deltas.
ChunkBrokenState stores a 16-byte header and 3-byte mined-block records.
A record packs local x, local z, and y offset. Capacity starts at 64, grows by 64, and caps at 2,048 records.
GlobalConfig world seed, chunk size, build height, max terrain height, and sea level are used for generated block verification.
notes: This is the current mining write path used by the browser client.
MagicBlock delegation hooks exist in the codebase, but the current gameplay path does not require MagicBlock.
Backpack resource settlement is intentionally separate so mining state and inventory state can evolve independently.

pdaAccounts.name: ChunkBrokenState
pdaAccounts.purpose: Sparse record of generated blocks broken by players.
fields: grows by capacity instead of storing full terrain

pdaAccounts.name: ResourceDropTable
pdaAccounts.purpose: Bounded extra-drop rules for rewarded mining.

programs.name: NiceChunk Guardian Registry
programs.role: Registers Guardian regions, endpoints, NCK treasury payment, liveness proof, settlement, and endpoint updates.
programs.summary: The guardian program records public service endpoints for regional realtime infrastructure. A guardian registers a region, pays the NCK registration amount to the treasury token account, and periodically submits proofs. The registry is non-custodial and does not sign player transactions.
storage: Registry stores GlobalConfig, NCK mint, treasury token account, active count, and genesis flag.
Region stores status, region coordinates, owner/operator wallet, endpoint host, port, TLS flag, stake amount, slash total, proof count, and timestamps.
Region size is 100 by 100 chunks. Registration requires a 100,000 NCK treasury payment at 6 decimals.
Proof interval is 3,600 seconds. Settlement can slash 10% per missed proof window until removal.
notes: The current web Guardian page reads this registry and region PDA data from devnet.
Endpoint updates are limited to the guardian owner/operator flow.
The registration payment goes to the treasury token account; this registry does not provide user-initiated unstaking or withdrawal.

pdaAccounts.name: GuardianRegistry
pdaAccounts.purpose: Global Guardian registry and treasury pointer.

pdaAccounts.name: GuardianRegion
pdaAccounts.purpose: Registered realtime region endpoint and stake state.

programs.name: NiceChunk Backpack
programs.kind: Inventory slot layer for blocks, resources, and items
programs.role: Creates player backpacks, stores block-resource and item slots, removes selected slots, and accepts marketplace or smelting-authorized delivery.
programs.accountSize: BackpackAccount v2: 6,464 bytes; BackpackSlotRecord: 64 bytes; legacy resource record: 10 bytes
programs.summary: The backpack program is the inventory storage layer for mined block resources, smelted materials, forged items, and marketplace escrow. Version 2 stores fixed 64-byte slots that can represent either block resources or item/material records. Mining appends block-resource slots through validated player sessions, marketplace flows can remove and restore resources, and smelting can append item slots only through the smelting authority PDA.
instructions: requires a PlayerProfile with no equipped backpack
storage: Backpack header stores backpack ID, owner, capacity, item count, state, placement fields, timestamps, and flags.
Default capacity is 50 slots. Max capacity is 99 slots.
Each v2 BackpackSlotRecord is 64 bytes: kind, category, flags, quantity, resource coordinate, item code, item ID, item PDA, and volume.
Slot kind 1 stores block resources. Slot kind 2 stores item/material records such as smelted outputs or forged outputs.
The decoder still recognizes legacy 10-byte resource records for older backpack accounts.
notes: Backpack contents are separate from public player profile display; the player profile stores only the equipped backpack public key.
The marketplace program can escrow or deliver backpack resources through the market-authority PDA.
The smelting program can append material or item outputs through the smelting-authority PDA; users cannot mint arbitrary item slots directly.
Direct player appends remain restricted to validated mining sessions for block resources.

pdaAccounts.name: Backpack
pdaAccounts.purpose: Wallet-owned inventory PDA for block resources and item references.
fields: up to 99 slot records with block resource or item reference

programs.name: NiceChunk Smelting
programs.kind: Recipe table and backpack item creation layer
programs.role: Stores smelting recipe tables, consumes selected backpack resources and fuels, and appends material or item outputs back into the backpack.
programs.summary: The smelting program separates recipe authority from backpack storage. A recipe table PDA stores up to 12 recipes, each with up to 8 input slots and 4 output slots. Executing a recipe validates selected backpack inputs and fuel, removes those slots through the backpack program, then appends material or item outputs through the smelting authority PDA.
instructions: execute_smelting supports input indexes, fuel indexes, and a batch multiplier.
storage: RecipeTable stores table ID, authority, recipe count, timestamps, and fixed-size recipe records.
Each RecipeRecord stores recipe ID, enabled flag, minimum heat tier, input count, output count, up to 8 input BackpackSlot records, up to 4 output BackpackSlot records, and updated slot.
BackpackSlot records support block resources and item/material outputs.
Recipe table authority can upsert recipes or transfer authority.
notes: The recipe table is intentionally independent so civilization or governance systems can add and update recipes later.
Execution validates backpack ownership, recipe matching, input slots, fuel slots, and batch multiplier before consuming inventory.
Outputs are appended through the backpack program using the smelting authority PDA; users cannot mint arbitrary smelting outputs directly.

pdaAccounts.name: RecipeTable
pdaAccounts.purpose: Authority-owned smelting recipe table used by the smelting program.
fields: each recipe stores heat tier, input slots, output slots and updatedSlot

pdaAccounts.name: SmeltingAuthority
pdaAccounts.purpose: Program signer used to append outputs back into a backpack after recipe validation.

programs.name: NiceChunk Marketplace
programs.kind: Resource and asset marketplace settlement layer
programs.role: Creates asset accounts and listings, escrows backpack resources or Asset PDAs, settles NCK or SOL payment, charges the market fee, and returns or delivers listed inventory.
programs.summary: The marketplace program is the current devnet trading surface for backpack resources and market Assets. A seller can create a MarketAsset PDA for equipment, clothing, building materials, or other hotbar items, then create a listing PDA. Backpack listings remove the selected resource from the seller backpack; Asset listings lock the Asset account to the listing. Cancellation restores the resource or unlocks the Asset to the seller; purchase transfers SOL or NCK, sends a 1% market fee to the treasury, and delivers the resource or Asset ownership to the buyer.
instructions: Backpack listings require backpack CPI escrow and restore/delivery accounts.
Asset listings require a market-owned 256-byte MarketAsset PDA that is locked to the listing until cancel or purchase.
Payment supports NCK and SOL with a 1% market fee.
storage: Listing stores seller wallet, listing ID, category, currency, source kind, source slot, quantity, price, item hash, source inventory, timestamps, buyer, and final state.
Backpack listings store the source backpack public key and the 10-byte resource coordinate record.
MarketAsset v2 stores owner, asset ID, category, quantity, item hash, linked listing, timestamps, item code, stack count, durability, and up to 96 bytes of asset payload for forged item data.
Marketplace categories are raw materials, equipment, building materials, and clothing.
State is active, canceled, or sold for listings; active or listed for Assets.
notes: Only the listing seller can cancel an active listing.
Buyers cannot purchase their own listings.
Only the marketplace PDA can restore or deliver escrowed backpack resources through the backpack program.
MarketAsset ownership moves to the buyer when an asset listing is purchased.
Treasury fee is 1% of the listing price in the chosen currency.

pdaAccounts.name: MarketplaceListing
pdaAccounts.purpose: Seller-owned listing state for backpack resources or asset PDAs.
fields: sourceInventory, sourceRecord, buyer and sold fields

pdaAccounts.name: MarketAsset
pdaAccounts.purpose: Owned tradable asset record for equipment, building, clothing or raw assets.
fields: bounded payload bytes for dynamic item identity

pdaAccounts.name: MarketplaceAuthority
pdaAccounts.purpose: Program signer for escrow and settlement authority boundaries.
fields: Does not custody player wallets outside explicit instructions

chainBrowser.title: Read the public devnet state directly from RPC.
chainBrowser.body: Scan NiceChunk program accounts, decode known PDA layouts, and inspect players, backpacks, smelting tables, market listings, Guardian regions, and chunk deltas without trusting a private database.
chainBrowser.ready: Choose a PDA group and refresh to read public devnet state.
chainBrowser.loadingAll: Scanning all known NiceChunk PDA groups from devnet RPC...
chainBrowser.empty: No decoded accounts found for this group.
chainBrowser.programBody: Read public PDA accounts owned by this program. The scan is read-only and uses the configured Solana RPC endpoint.
chainBrowser.programPrompt: Choose a PDA group for this program.
chainBrowser.noKnownTypes: No known data PDA group for this program yet.
chainBrowser.timeout: RPC request timed out. Try again later or switch to a more stable RPC endpoint.
chainBrowser.searchPlaceholder: Filter by address, owner, item, or decoded JSON

all.title: All known PDA groups
all.body: Scans each decoded NiceChunk account family exposed by this page. This is a public RPC read, not a backend export.
all.visible: Program owner, PDA address, decoded header fields, and safe samples from bounded account data.
all.trust: Lets players compare the website, source code, PDA layout, and live devnet state from one screen.

global-config.title: GlobalConfig
global-config.body: The immutable genesis configuration for this devnet world.
global-config.visible: NCK mint, world ID, world seed hash material, config hashes, chunk limits, and guardian parameters.
global-config.trust: Shows the world is not secretly changing seed, resource config, or core limits from a private server.

player-profile.title: PlayerProfile
player-profile.visible: Owner wallet, position, attributes, equipment summary, equipped backpack pointer, and update slots.
player-profile.trust: Players can see which identities exist and which backpack each public profile points to.

player-session.title: PlayerSession
player-session.body: Short-lived authority used to reduce repeated wallet prompts during allowed gameplay actions.
player-session.visible: Owner, session authority, allowed action flags, expiration, max actions, and used action count.
player-session.trust: Shows that session power is bounded instead of an unlimited hidden operator key.

chunk-broken.title: ChunkBrokenState
chunk-broken.body: Compact per-chunk mining record for blocks that players have already broken.
chunk-broken.visible: Version, capacity, minY, count, and sample packed broken-block records.
chunk-broken.trust: Makes mined terrain changes auditable without storing full terrain on-chain.

resource-drop-table.title: ResourceDropTable
resource-drop-table.body: Bounded resource drop rule table used by mining rewards.
resource-drop-table.visible: Source block, drop block, chance basis points, altitude/depth bounds, and salt.
resource-drop-table.trust: Lets players inspect drop rules instead of trusting opaque reward logic.

guardian-registry.title: GuardianRegistry
guardian-registry.body: Global registry for realtime service coverage.
guardian-registry.visible: Treasury token, active count, registrations, stake amount, slash amount, and region size.
guardian-registry.trust: Shows service economics and coverage rules are public PDA data.

guardian-region.title: GuardianRegion
guardian-region.body: Registered realtime region endpoint and proof state.
guardian-region.visible: Region bounds, owner, operator, endpoint, TLS flag, stake, penalties, and proof counters.
guardian-region.trust: Players can verify who serves a region and whether liveness proofs are being submitted.

backpack.title: Backpack
backpack.body: Player-owned inventory PDA for block resources, material slots, and item references.
backpack.visible: Owner, backpack ID, capacity, item count, placement, update slots, and safe sample slots.
backpack.trust: Inventory is inspectable from public account data instead of only from the game UI.

recipe-table.title: RecipeTable
recipe-table.body: Authority-owned smelting recipe table used by the smelting program.
recipe-table.visible: Authority, recipe count, update slot, heat tiers, input counts, output counts, and recipe IDs.
recipe-table.trust: Players can inspect which recipes exist before spending backpack resources.

market-listing.title: MarketplaceListing
market-listing.body: Public listing state for backpack resources or market assets.
market-listing.visible: Seller, listing ID, category, currency, source kind, quantity, price, buyer, and final state.
market-listing.trust: Market escrow and settlement can be audited without trusting a private order book.

market-asset.title: MarketAsset
market-asset.body: Owned tradable asset account for equipment, materials, clothing, or item payloads.
market-asset.visible: Owner, asset ID, state, category, quantity, item hash, linked listing, item code, and bounded payload.
market-asset.trust: Assets have explicit owners and listing state that can be checked before purchase.

forged-items.title: Forged item records
forged-items.body: A convenience scan that finds forged outputs inside live Backpack and MarketAsset accounts.
forged-items.visible: Source account type, owner, backpack or asset identity, forged sample slots, item code, item ID, and payload where available.
forged-items.trust: Shows forged results through real account data while staying honest that a dedicated forging PDA family is not yet live.

transparency.title: Players can audit the world from contract code to public PDA state.
transparency.body: NiceChunk treats public state as part of the game interface. Players should be able to inspect who exists, which backpacks and assets exist, what recipes are active, which regions serve realtime play, and which rules or accounts are still only planned.

cards.title: Players are public account records, not hidden rows in a private database.
cards.body: PlayerProfile and PlayerSession PDAs expose the wallet, position, equipped backpack pointer, session authority, and limits that the client is allowed to use.

cards.title: Backpacks and market assets are readable storage objects.
cards.body: Backpack, MarketplaceListing, and MarketAsset accounts show item counts, owners, states, prices, and sample item payloads so players can audit inventory movement.
points: Forged items surface through item slots or asset payloads.

cards.title: Terrain and mining state are tied to verifiable world config.
cards.body: GlobalConfig fixes the world seed and config hashes, while ChunkBrokenState and ResourceDropTable expose what has been mined and which extra-drop rules exist.
points: World seed and hashes are public.
Broken-block records are compact but readable.
Drop tables are bounded PDA data.

cards.title: Governance should be visible before it becomes powerful.
cards.body: The current contracts expose the surfaces that future civilization rules must bind to: recipe tables, resource rules, guardians, market policy, and player-owned assets.
points: Live surfaces are separated from planned ones.
Configuration changes should target PDA data.
Old player assets need stronger protection.

map.audit: Read PlayerProfile accounts to see wallets, positions, equipment slots, and equipped backpack pointers.
Read Backpack accounts to inspect owners, capacity, item counts, block resources, and item/material slots.
Read forged records from Backpack item slots and MarketAsset payloads without inventing a separate live forging program.
Read RecipeTable accounts to inspect active recipe tables, heat tiers, input counts, output counts, and update slots.
Read GuardianRegion and GuardianRegistry accounts to audit realtime region owners, endpoints, stake, proof counts, and penalties.
Read listings and market assets to inspect seller, buyer, price, currency, escrow source, state, and settlement slots.
